review_notes: The functions are applicable to Kubernetes as they relate to detecting and preventing unauthorized changes, which aligns with the withdrawn NIST control CM-6(3). The original function names were mostly clear but required slight adjustments for consistency with naming conventions and clarity. The use of audit logging, admission controllers, and RBAC are appropriate mappings to the control's intent.
naming_improvements: Use 'unauthorized_change_prevention' for RBAC to better reflect the function's purpose
title: Configuration Settings - Configuration settings | unauthorized change detection [withdrawn
step1_reasoning: The control CM-6(3) focuses on detecting unauthorized changes, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring audit logging is configured to capture changes, using admission controllers to prevent unauthorized changes, and enforcing RBAC policies to manage who can make changes. These measures collectively help in detecting and preventing unauthorized changes in a Kubernetes environment.
